decisions.decision: Deferred the cleanup in-code trust gate rather than auto-applying isSyncTrusted
decisions.rationale: On a schedule event isSyncTrusted requires repository.default_branch from GITHUB_EVENT_PATH + GITHUB_REF_NAME===default_branch; it is UNVERIFIED that the schedule-event payload reliably provides repository.default_branch (publish only exercises isSyncTrusted on push). If it does not, the gate returns not-default-branch and cleanup SILENTLY no-ops forever, leaking toward the 1000-asset cap and violating the retention-locked requirement. This is the exact catastrophe retention.ts guards.

blocking_constraints.name: isSyncTrusted-on-schedule may fail-closed
blocking_constraints.detail: Do NOT wire isSyncTrusted into cleanup run() until a test proves it returns {trusted:true} on a real schedule-event payload. A fail-closed gate silently disables scheduled cleanup => retention leak. See memory cleanup-in-code-trust-gate-deferred and .planning/STATE.md.
